Copper - Regime State

A Deterministic Summary of Copper Macro Regime Conditions.

As of 2026-03-07

Gemini Summary

### Copper Regime State — Deterministic Summary
Dimension State Primary Driver / Conflict
Primary Macro Regime Cyclical Contraction (Stress-Led) Dominance of Elevated Stress and Volatility over Neutral Growth/Liquidity (11)(13).
Growth & Demand Phase Neutral Industrial demand signals are mixed; Pricing is deteriorating but structural expansion indicators (PMI/China) are absent/neutral (7)(14).
Liquidity Regime Neutral Restrictive Real Rates (1) are offset by Neutral Fed Liquidity (2) and M2 growth (3).
USD Conditioning Weakening USD Index persists below 12-month moving average despite fading negative momentum (5).
Stress & Risk Appetite Elevated VIX spike (29.49) and Financial Stress Index Z-score (0.67) trigger risk-off dominance (13)(11).
Positioning & Flows Bearish Reversal Risk Price/Flow divergence; Speculative long reduction occurring alongside rising producer hedge pressure (7)(9).
Market Expression Emerging Weakness Broad price breakdowns below technical averages across FCX, SFR, and Wire proxies (14)(16).

Horizon Bias

1-Month HorizonBearish
3-Month HorizonNeutral

System Flags

Conflict FlagYes (USD vs Stress)
Stress OverrideActive

Regime Reasoning & Dominance

  • Stress Override: Per Rule 6, "Elevated" stress in the VIX (3.15 z-score) (13) and St. Louis Fed Index (11) mandates that cyclical risk-off logic dominates all other signals.
  • Growth/Liquidity Neutrality: Neither Growth nor Liquidity meet the 2+ Bullish/Bearish criteria for a directional regime, leaving the asset sensitive to exogenous stress shocks (2)(3).
  • Flow Asymmetry: Technical Exhaustion (10) and a negative divergence between rising prices and falling speculative conviction (7) create downside positioning risk.
  • USD Conflict: A "Weakening" USD Index (5) provides a tailwind that prevents a "Contractionary" Growth classification, maintaining the primary regime as "Neutral" before the Stress Override is applied.
The information presented is for general informational purposes only and does not constitute financial or investment advice. It has been prepared without regard to individual objectives, financial situation, or needs. You should consider whether it is appropriate for your circumstances and seek independent advice where necessary.
